sglang.title: [Bug] Catch any errors caused by parsing json schema
sglang.author: zolinthecow
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Passing json_schema with types unimplemented by Outlines to the /generate endpoint would crash the scheduler and take down the whole server.
Example:
skip invalid json schema: json_schema={"type":"object","properties":{"id":{"type":"string","format":"uuid"},"name":{"type":"string","minLen

sglang.title: [Fix] Fix NaN issues by fixing the cuda graph padding values for flashinfer
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, we set seq_len = 0 for padded requests. This can result in some NaN issues, as reported in #1571.
This PR fixes it by setting the seq_len = 1. This mostly only happens for models with dynamic fp8 quantization --quantization fp8, so I suspect it relates to the dynamic quantization and fla

sglang.title: [Fix] Fix cuda graph padding for triton attention backend
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
We should initialize req_to_token with torch.zeros instead of torch.empty to avoid out-of-bound access during cuda graph capture.
Then we do not need get_cuda_graph_seq_len_fill_value. We can always use 1.

sglang.title: check user-specified model_max_len with hf derived max_model_len
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR aims to improve the handling of context length in the ModelConfig class. It addresses potential issues when users specify a context length exceeding the model's derived maximum, enhances error messaging, and introduces a controlled override mechanism. These changes will prevent de

sglang.title: Re-introduce `get_cuda_graph_seq_len_fill_value`
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt: Revert some changes on get_cuda_graph_seq_len_fill_value in #1782. We find this still useful in some cases.

sglang.title: Create deploy-docs.yml
sglang.author: zhaochenyang20
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Modifications

Checklist

 Format your code according to the Contributor Guide.
 Add unit tests as outlined in the Contributor Guide.
 Update documentation as needed, including docstrings or example tutorials.

sglang.title: Enhance the test case for chunked prefill and check memory leak
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Add the infra to test there is no memory leak from the server log

Currently this is turned off because there is actually a bug in the code. Will turn on this assert after we fix the bug.


Fix the hanging issue in CI when we use subprocess.Popen(stderr=subprocess.PIPE)
Split the unit tests into fou

sglang.title: Fix seq_lens_sum for cuda graph runner in padded cases
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
When we compute seq_lens_sum for cuda graph, we need to consider padded requests.
This fixed the error for https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/actions/runs/11510875908/job/32043401541

vllm.title: [core] simplify seq group code
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: many if-else condition will be dead after #9302 . we can prune the code.

vllm.title: [Doc] Move additional tips/notes to the top

vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently, the instructions for using modelscope and checking model support are buried under the long list of supported models. This PR moves them to the top of the page for better visibility.

vllm.title: [Bugfix]Disable the post_norm layer of the vision encoder for LLaVA models
vllm.author: litianjian
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Disable the post_norm layer of vision encoder for LLaVA-Onevision models based on #9217.

vllm.title: Increase operation per run limit for "Close inactive issues and PRs" workflow
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Increases operations-per-run from 30 to 1000.
This ensures that changes to this workflow propagate to all issues and PRs in days rather than months.
In the most recent run (https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/actions/runs/11491223727/job/31983238963#step:2:19447) you can see that there are ~5k oper

vllm.title: [torch.compile] Adding torch compile annotations to some models
vllm.author: CRZbulabula
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR is to improve #9589, adding torch compile annotations to some models and testing them.

vllm.title: [CI/Build] Fix VLM test failures when using transformers v4.46
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes the test failures in VLM tests:

Temporary skip Chameleon tests as the model is unusable in transformers v4.46.0 (huggingface/transformers#34379)
Fix wrap_device not working on nested dictionaries causing MiniCPM-V tests to fail
Fix dtype mismatch in PaliGemma tests

vllm.title: [Model] Compute Llava Next Max Tokens / Dummy Data From Gridpoints
vllm.author: alex-jw-brooks
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, the LLava-next max token per images count & dummy data is calculated using a fixed height and width that picks the highest feature tile layout for the default image grid points in most llava-next models. I.e., given grid pinpoints:
[[336, 672], [672, 336], [672, 672], [1008, 336], [336, 1

vllm.title: [Log][Bugfix] Fix default value check for `image_url.detail`
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The warning about not supporting image_url.detail is overzealous as it doesn't deal with detail == None, which can be the default case. This PR simply has None represented as "auto" by default, which matches OpenAI: https://github.com/openai/openai-python/blob/e1b2f8216cc69e802475a0d438e40e0e74510de

vllm.title: [Performance][Kernel] Fused_moe Performance Improvement
vllm.author: charlifu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR tries to improve the performance of vllm fuse moe layer by:

Increase the thread number of moe_align_block_size_kernel to warp size.
Replace torch.sum with customized moe_sum.
Move moe_align_block_size_kernels.cu to moe/moe_align_sum_kernels.cu.

Motivations:

moe_align_block_size_kernel onl

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Remove xformers requirement for Pixtral
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds a torch SDPA implementation for PixtralHF attention
FIX #9036 (comment)

I'm trying to run LlavaNextForConditionalGeneration on a non-CUDA hardware platform, but when I import llava_next, it pulls in pixtral, which in turn imports xformers. From my understanding, xformers is required on

vllm.title: [ci/Build] Skip Chameleon for transformers 4.46.0 on broadcast test #9675
vllm.author: khluu
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This temporarily skips Chameleon test in test_broadcast since the model is not working with transformers v4.46.0

vllm.title: [Model] add a lora module for granite 3.0 MoE models
vllm.author: willmj
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
all-linear option for new granite-3.0 MoE models produces these target modules:
  "target_modules": [
    "q_proj",
    "v_proj",
    "k_proj",
    "layer",
    "o_proj"
  ],

This PR adds the missing module layer to the supported_lora_modules in granitemoe.
BEFORE SUBMITTING, PLEASE READ THE CHECKL

vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR ports the change in #9403 to support sliding window attention with vllm-flash-attn on V1.
